title: Daily Briefing — Saturday, May 23
tldr: OpenAI's internal model disproved an 80-year-old math conjecture (the Erdős unit-distance problem) with a 125-page proof — Fields medalist Tim Gowers called it “a milestone in AI mathematics.”
OpenAI filed for IPO; Anthropic reported its first-ever profit (~$10.9B revenue), and a SpaceX S-1 revealed a ~$1.25B/month Anthropic compute deal.

Governance whiplash: the White House scrapped its AI safety executive order after calls from Zuckerberg, Musk, and Sacks; leaked audio showed Meta tracking employees to train AI the same day it cut ~8,000 staff.

blocks.p: An internal OpenAI reasoning model autonomously disproved the Erdős unit-distance conjecture using algebraic number theory (a 125-page proof). Mathematician Tim Gowers called it a milestone.
blocks.why: Models are crossing from “solving known problems” to “producing new knowledge” — the leading indicator everyone watches.
blocks.doIt: If you do quantitative or research work, start testing frontier models on genuinely open problems, not just textbook tasks.

blocks.p: OpenAI filed for IPO; Anthropic reported its first operating profit on ~$10.9B revenue; a SpaceX S-1 disclosed a ~$1.25B/month compute commitment to Anthropic.
blocks.why: Labs are becoming scaled, public-market businesses — and compute contracts are now balance-sheet-sized.

blocks.p: The Trump White House canceled a planned AI safety executive order (a 90-day model-review framework) after direct lobbying from Zuckerberg, Musk, and Sacks. Separately, leaked audio showed Meta had tracked employees' Gmail, coding, and tools to train AI — the same day it laid off ~8,000.
blocks.why: Don't bank on near-term US federal AI rules; plan around state law (CA, CO) and the EU timeline.

sources: buildfastwithai (May 23 roundup) · OpenAI · Anthropic · CNBC · The Information (several figures reported same-day; treat as directional).
